trade.summary: The Netherlands is the UK's second-largest export destination and among its most important trading partners. Rotterdam is the primary entry point for UK goods into the EU. UK exports financial services, chemicals, and agri-food to the Netherlands. The Netherlands re-exports and ships vast quantities of goods to the UK. Post-Brexit, Dutch ports became crucial EU-UK trade intermediaries.
disputes: post-Brexit customs friction at Rotterdam-Felixstowe corridor

diplomatic.summary: The UK and the Netherlands have exceptionally close ties — shared monarchy history (House of Orange), financial links (Shell, Unilever Anglo-Dutch mergers), and aligned foreign policy. The Netherlands has been among the more pragmatic EU members on post-Brexit UK relations. Both countries closely aligned on NATO, Ukraine, and Russia sanctions. The Hague hosts the ICC, relevant to various UK international law positions.
recent_incidents: Ajax-Tottenham fan violence in Amsterdam (2024) — brief diplomatic moment

military.summary: Both NATO allies with deep military cooperation. UK-Netherlands Amphibious Force is a combined military unit. Close intelligence sharing (Netherlands AIVD works closely with MI6/GCHQ). Joint naval patrols and North Sea operations. Both countries strongly aligned on NATO's eastern flank and Ukraine support.

financial.summary: Extraordinarily deep financial ties. Shell (historically Anglo-Dutch, now UK-headquartered) and Unilever represent the depth of bilateral corporate integration. Amsterdam became the largest European equity trading venue post-Brexit (overtaking London for EU shares). Significant Dutch institutional investment in UK gilts and equities. ASML's chip lithography monopoly is of strategic interest to UK tech policy.

energy.summary: North Sea energy cooperation through joint gas fields and pipeline infrastructure. Netherlands was historically a major gas exporter to UK through Bacton-Zeebrugge interconnector. Both are transitioning away from fossil fuels. Cooperation in offshore wind and hydrogen corridors.

scientific.summary: Very strong research ties. Major Dutch universities (Leiden, Amsterdam, Delft) have deep UK institutional links. CERN, ESA participation overlaps. Life sciences corridor (Oxford-Cambridge-Amsterdam-Leiden) is a global hub. UK retained Horizon Europe access restoring joint research funding.
